summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates filesystem contents and git history to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js spawns /bin/sh to recursively read every file under /app and dump full git log/show output, then transmits the harvested data in 240-byte chunks via HTTP PUT to a hardcoded remote endpoint at http://154.57.164.71:31289. The script specifically searches for and uploads HTB{...} flag patterns alongside the captured files. While the script also references loopback/CTF-style targets as decoys, the public IP is a live exfiltration channel that fires automatically during dependency installation, before any code review or sandboxing is possible.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
